title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.title: Kuzu Tandir Recipe: Authentic Turkish Slow Roasted Lamb
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Kuzu Tandir with our authentic Turkish slow roasted lamb recipe. Tender, flavorful, and perfect for a special dinner. Get the step-by-step guide!
longTailKeywords: easy Kuzu Tandir recipe at home
Kuzu Tandir step by step guide
seo.h1: Authentic Kuzu Tandir: Turkish Slow Roasted Lamb Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Kuzu Tandir
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Kuzu Tandir
Pro Tips for the Best Kuzu Tandir Results
Variations and Substitutions for Kuzu Tandir
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully plated Kuzu Tandir, authentic Turkish slow roasted lamb, garnished with fresh herbs and spices.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of Turkey with this succulent Kuzu Tandir, a slow roasted lamb dish perfect for any special occasion.

description: Kuzu Tandir is a celebrated Turkish dish featuring succulent lamb, slow-roasted to perfection until it is incredibly tender and falls off the bone. This traditional recipe captures the essence of Anatolian cuisine, offering rich, aromatic flavors with minimal effort. It is an ideal centerpiece for family gatherings or special occasions, promising a truly memorable dining experience.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, mix olive oil, salt, black pepper, paprika, cumin, and oregano to create a paste. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into any crevices. You can also make a few shallow cuts in the lamb to allow the seasoning to penetrate deeper.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb (Optional)
instructions.text: In a large, oven-safe pot or Dutch oven, heat a little extra olive oil over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until nicely browned. This step is optional but adds depth of flavor and a beautiful crust to the meat. Remove the lamb and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare for Roasting
instructions.text: Add the chopped onion wedges and smashed garlic cloves to the same pot. Saute for 2-3 minutes until fragrant. Place the seared lamb back into the pot, resting it on top of the onions and garlic. Pour the hot water or lamb broth around the lamb.

instructions.title: Slow Roast in the Oven
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Cover the pot tightly with its lid or with aluminum foil if using a roasting pan. Transfer to the preheated oven and slow roast for 3.5 to 4 hours, or until the lamb is incredibly tender and easily pulls away from the bone with a fork. Check occasionally to ensure liquid has not fully evaporated, adding a little more if needed.

instructions.title: Rest the Lamb
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the pot from the oven. Let the lamb rest, still covered, for at least 15-20 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in an even more tender and flavorful meat. The resting period is crucial for perfect Kuzu Tandir.

instructions.title: Serve Kuzu Tandir
instructions.text: Uncover the lamb. The meat should be so tender that it can be easily pulled apart with two forks or gently sliced. Serve the Kuzu Tandir immediately with the pan juices. It pairs wonderfully with pilaf (rice), roasted vegetables, or a simple green salad.

tips: For even more flavor, marinate the lamb overnight in the spice rub. This allows the spices to deeply penetrate the meat.
If you dont have a Dutch oven, you can use a deep roasting pan tightly covered with heavy-duty aluminum foil to trap the moisture effectively.
Serve Kuzu Tandir with a side of traditional Turkish bulgur pilaf or plain rice and a dollop of strained yogurt (süzme yoğurt) for a complete meal.
article: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb is a beloved Turkish Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
